body.title: [sprint] Constraint normalizer: stamp constraints into ticket at add time
body.intention: I intend that every ticket has applicable constraints pre-stamped into it at add/audit time so sprint agents see zero discovery cost — constraints are already there when the ticket is opened.
body.description:
Currently sprint agents must read CLAUDE.md, palace rules, and ticket design rules fields manually — expensive in tokens and easy to miss. The decorator runs at ticket-add time (background, no LLM) and stamps the output of get_constraints() directly into the ticket.

Integration point: cc_queue.py add pipeline, after Scraps validation. Also runs on audit-ticket.

What gets stamped into the ticket (appended to description as a fenced block):
  ## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
  [hard_block] No SQLite — applies: **/*.py (source: CLAUDE.md)
  [error] Log every interface crossing — applies: devices/* (source: palace/rules/coding)
  ...

The LLM opening the ticket sees these already. No MCP call needed at sprint time.

This is the same hook pattern as Scraps validation — lightweight background call, result embedded in ticket, transparent to the sprint agent.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (add pipeline); devices/hubert/constraint_decorator.py (new — calls get_constraints, formats stamp)
**Design rules:** external-state; decoration is idempotent (re-stamp replaces previous block); no SQLite
**Scope boundary:** IN — call get_constraints at add/audit time; stamp block into ticket description. OUT — building the constraints store (T-constraint-normalizer-store); orientation decoration (pre_inference_assemble.py — separate concern).
**Completion criteria:** After cc_queue.py add, ticket description contains ## Pre-computed constraints block with >=1 constraint entry; re-running add on same ticket replaces not duplicates the block; block is absent when get_constraints returns empty.
